title: S.A.M Crafting Recipe
description: Makes one of your custom items craftable at a bench. Every recipe must name the bench it belongs on with 'kit': either the framework's built-in Hunter's Workbench, or one of your own items turned into a bench. The vanilla tinkering kit cannot be changed by a mod - its recipes, prices and cells are always exactly what the game ships. A bench has a 5x4 = 20 cell grid, and a custom bench gets all 20 to itself.

$schema.description: Optional. URL of this JSON Schema, so editors give autocomplete and validation.

id.description: Optional. A 'namespace:name' id for this recipe itself. Only used in log messages; the recipe is keyed by its item and its kit.

item.description: The item this recipe produces, as one of your own custom items: 'namespace:item'. A vanilla item cannot be given a recipe, because that would change the vanilla tinkering kit.

kit.description: Required. The bench this recipe appears on. Use 'sam:hunters_workbench' for the framework's built-in bench, which every player already has and no mod needs to ship. Or name one of your own custom items to make it a bench of its own: using it from the inventory opens a grid showing only the recipes bound to it. A recipe with no kit appears nowhere.

materials.description: Optional. Pay for this craft with your OWN items instead of metal and magic scrap. The crafting panel has room for two material columns, so you may name up to two. Omit this to use the scrap costs below.

item.description: The material consumed, as 'namespace:item'. Give that item an 'icon' so the crafting panel can show it; without one the panel falls back to the scrap icon.

count.description: How many of this material a single craft consumes.

metal_cost.description: Metal scrap (TOOL_METAL_SCRAP) consumed per craft. Ignored when 'materials' is used. Without materials, metal_cost + magic_cost must be at least 1: a recipe that costs nothing can never be crafted, because the engine's affordability check bails when both are zero.

magic_cost.description: Magic scrap (TOOL_MAGIC_SCRAP) consumed per craft. Ignored when 'materials' is used.

skill_level.description: Tinkering skill TIER required to craft this, 0 to 5. Each tier is 20 points of (Tinkering skill + PER), so the tiers are 0/20/40/60/80/100. Tiers are used rather than a raw number because the crafting screen displays the requirement as tier x 20.

status.description: Condition the crafted item comes out at. Defaults to EXCELLENT. Note the game's spelling of SERVICABLE.

slot.description: Optional. Pin this recipe to an exact cell in the bench's 5x4 grid. Omit it and the recipe drops into the next free cell, which is usually what you want. If the pinned cell is already taken, the next free cell is used instead.
